title: Fix OCR date extraction from uploaded medical report documents

requirements.summary: Expand date extraction regex patterns in ocrExtraction.ts to reliably detect report dates from medical documents in multiple formats (DD/MM/YYYY, MM/DD/YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, DD-MM-YYYY, DD MMM YYYY, MMM DD YYYY) and with common label prefixes (Date:, Report Date:, Collection Date:, Sample Date:, Test Date:, Date of Report:), returning a valid ISO date string (YYYY-MM-DD) that pre-populates the date field in UploadRecordForm
acceptanceCriteria: Uploading a medical report PDF or image with a date in any of the supported formats results in the date field being correctly pre-populated in the review form.
Dates prefixed with labels like 'Date:', 'Report Date:', 'Collection Date:', 'Sample Date:', 'Test Date:', or 'Date of Report:' are correctly parsed.
Unlabelled dates in common formats (DD/MM/YYYY, MM/DD/YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, DD-MM-YYYY, DD MMM YYYY, MMM DD YYYY) are detected and returned.
The extracted date is converted to a valid ISO 8601 string (YYYY-MM-DD) before being set in the form state.
If no date is found, the date field remains empty (no crash or invalid value is set).
All previously passing OCR extraction tests for patient name and test categories continue to pass.

file_operations.description: Expand the extractDate function regex patterns to match multiple common date formats including DD/MM/YYYY, MM/DD/YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, DD-MM-YYYY, DD MMM YYYY (e.g., 12 Jan 2024), and MMM DD, YYYY (e.g., Jan 12, 2024). Add support for label-prefixed date formats such as 'Date:', 'Report Date:', 'Collection Date:', 'Sample Date:', 'Test Date:', and 'Date of Report:'. Implement robust date parsing logic that converts matched date strings to valid ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD), handling month name abbreviations and different separator characters. Ensure the function returns undefined if no valid date is found, and add validation to prevent invalid date values from being returned.
